evals.name: new-cja-appointment-full-workflow
evals.prompt: I just got appointed on a CJA case, United States v. Marcus Doe. The government's first discovery production is in the folder ./US-v-Doe (the indictment is in there too). Get me up to speed on this case: build me a discovery tracker, deal with any recordings, and take a first pass through the discovery telling me what the evidence shows on each charge. Keep everything local.
evals.expected_output: An Excel discovery tracker inventorying the produced items; handling of the bodycam recording (transcript or inventory note that transcription tooling is needed); a persistent case-memory markdown doc that breaks the two counts (21 USC 841 distribution; 18 USC 922(g)(1) felon-in-possession) into elements and maps evidence to each; flags for the exculpatory Jane Roe statement, the Fourth Amendment suppression hooks (air-freshener pretext stop / 35-min prolonged detention), and the items referenced but not produced (911 call recording, Officer Reyes bodycam). No fabricated Bates numbers or facts; nothing uploaded.

assertions.text: Produces an Excel (.xlsx) discovery tracker file in the outputs
Tracker inventories at least 6 of the produced items (arrest report, supplemental report, lab report, witness statement, bodycam, production letter)
Produces a persistent case-memory markdown document
Case memory breaks out BOTH counts: 21 U.S.C. 841 distribution of cocaine base AND 18 U.S.C. 922(g)(1) felon in possession, with their elements
Inventories the bodycam recording (bodycam_BWC-001.m4a) and either transcribes it or notes transcription tooling is needed, rather than ignoring it
Flags the exculpatory Jane Roe statement that someone other than the defendant placed the bag under the seat
Flags a Fourth Amendment / suppression hook (air-freshener pretext stop and/or ~35-minute prolonged roadside detention)
Identifies referenced-but-not-produced items: the 911 call recording and Officer Reyes's body-worn camera
Does not fabricate Bates numbers, holdings, or facts absent from the materials
Operates locally and does not attempt to upload or transmit discovery to any external service

evals.name: charge-map-and-missing-discovery
evals.prompt: Review the discovery in ./US-v-Doe against the indictment. I want a charge-by-charge breakdown of what helps us and what hurts us, plus a list of anything the discovery references that the government hasn't actually produced yet. Note any inconsistencies you spot.
evals.expected_output: A charge-by-charge analysis of both counts citing specific files; a list of missing/referenced-but-not-produced items (911 call recording, Officer Reyes bodycam, and reasonably the CI reliability/payment docs and analyst proficiency/calibration logs); identification of the consent inconsistency between Officer Kowalski's report (verbal consent to search) and Officer Reyes's supplemental report (search incident to arrest); and the exculpatory Jane Roe statement.

assertions.text: Breaks the indictment into both counts and their elements
For each count, states what evidence supports and what undercuts it, citing specific source files
Lists referenced-but-not-produced items including the 911 call recording and Officer Reyes's bodycam
Flags the consent inconsistency: Kowalski reports verbal consent to search while Reyes reports search incident to arrest
Flags the exculpatory Jane Roe statement
Does not fabricate facts, Bates numbers, or legal holdings not supported by the materials
